args.title: Finance Reconciliation Copilot
args.problem: Small finance teams lose time reconciling ecommerce orders, payment payouts, and accounting invoices across disconnected systems.
args.solution: A daily reconciliation assistant connects Shopify, Stripe, and QuickBooks, flags mismatches, and prepares approval-ready summaries.
args.targetUsers: Small finance and operations teams at ecommerce companies.
args.constraints: Start with Shopify, Stripe, and QuickBooks. Keep the first version focused on daily review workflows.
args.successCriteria: Finance users can review mismatches each morning and approve a clear summary with evidence.
openQuestions: Which accounting edge cases matter most in the first release?

entries.text: {"scores":{"specificity":5,"completeness":5,"researchability":5,"noInventedDetails":5},"passed":true,"notes":"The summary is specific enough for research and planning.","followUpQuestions":[]}
I found the order and need approval before issuing the refund.

args.reason: Damaged item arrived with a cracked base.

entries.text: I refunded order A-1001 for the damaged lamp and prepared the customer update.

{"sections":[{"title":"Market demand","question":"What demand signals support daily reconciliation for small ecommerce finance teams?","searchQueries":["small ecommerce finance reconciliation order payout invoice demand","finance teams daily reconciliation mismatch approval summary"]},{"title":"Competitive landscape","question":"Which products or approaches compete on reconciliation automation?","searchQueries":["reconciliation automation connector coverage mismatch detection tools","finance automation approval workflow audit trail competitors"]},{"title":"Pricing and workflow norms","question":"How do finance workflow buyers evaluate and price reconciliation automation?","searchQueries":["finance workflow automation pricing connected accounts transaction volume","exception review approval summary month end close finance teams"]},{"title":"Risks and implementation caveats","question":"What risks could block reconciliation automation adoption?","searchQueries":["reconciliation automation data quality integration mapping risks","financial record automation security retention least privilege risks"]}]}
Market demand points to daily reconciliation pain.
Approval-summary expectations also appear in finance workflow norms.
Competitors differentiate through connectors and workflows.
Audit trails are a buying criterion.
Pricing commonly scales with accounts and transaction volume.
Workflow depth and auditability matter for finance buyers.
Data quality and mapping are implementation risks.
Security reviews focus on access and retention controls.

{"summary":"Small ecommerce finance teams show a concrete daily reconciliation workflow with exception review pressure.","claims":[{"text":"Small finance teams have a daily reconciliation workflow across orders, payouts, and invoices.","citations":[{"sourceId":"src_market","quote":"Small finance teams reconcile commerce orders, payment processor payouts, and accounting invoices every morning."}]},{"text":"Approval summaries and exportable evidence are expected in finance review workflows.","citations":[{"sourceId":"src_pricing","quote":"Finance teams expect exception review queues, approval summaries, and exportable evidence for month-end close."}]}],"risks":["Demand must be validated by system-specific volume and close-cycle urgency."]}

{"summary":"The competitive field rewards products that combine connectors, explainability, workflow, and auditability.","claims":[{"text":"Reconciliation automation competes on connector breadth, mismatch explainability, and approval workflows.","citations":[{"sourceId":"src_competitors","quote":"Automation tools compete on connector coverage, explainable mismatch detection, and approval workflows."}]},{"text":"Finance buyers value an audit trail for reviewed exceptions.","citations":[{"sourceId":"src_competitors","quote":"Finance buyers prefer products that preserve an audit trail for every reviewed exception."}]}],"risks":["A thin connector set could make the product look narrower than established workflow tools."]}

{"summary":"Buying and packaging should map to connected systems, transaction volume, and depth of automation.","claims":[{"text":"Pricing norms can scale with connected accounts, transaction volume, and workflow automation depth.","citations":[{"sourceId":"src_pricing","quote":"Pricing commonly scales with connected accounts, transaction volume, and workflow automation depth."}]},{"text":"Exception review queues are part of expected finance workflow value.","citations":[{"sourceId":"src_pricing","quote":"Finance teams expect exception review queues, approval summaries, and exportable evidence for month-end close."}]}],"risks":["Usage-based pricing needs transparent limits so small teams do not fear runaway costs."]}

{"summary":"Implementation risk centers on source-system mappings, data quality, security, and retention controls.","claims":[{"text":"Data quality and integration mapping are recurring risks for reconciliation automation.","citations":[{"sourceId":"src_risks","quote":"Data quality and integration mapping are recurring risks for reconciliation automation projects."}]},{"text":"Security reviews focus on least privilege, encryption, and retention controls for financial records.","citations":[{"sourceId":"src_risks","quote":"Security reviews focus on least-privilege access, encryption, and clear retention controls for financial records."}]}],"risks":["The first release should constrain supported systems and document data handling clearly."]}

{"title":"PRD - Finance Reconciliation Copilot","requirements":[{"id":"req-1","text":"Connect Shopify, Stripe, and QuickBooks to reconcile orders, payouts, and invoices each morning.","acceptanceCriteria":["A user can connect the three systems in a test workspace.","The system produces a daily reconciliation run."]},{"id":"req-2","text":"Detect and explain mismatches between commerce, payment, and accounting records.","acceptanceCriteria":["Mismatch rows include source evidence and reason codes.","Users can filter by severity and system."]},{"id":"req-3","text":"Produce approval summaries with an auditable evidence trail.","acceptanceCriteria":["A reviewer can approve or reject a summary.","Each approval links back to source records and reviewer notes."]}],"sections":[{"heading":"Users and problem","body":"Finance users need a daily requirement for reconciliation because success depends on catching risk before close.","sourceIds":["src_market"],"requirementIds":["req-1","req-2"]},{"heading":"Goals and success metrics","body":"Success means fewer manual review hours, faster mismatch resolution, and a clear approval summary for each daily run.","sourceIds":["src_pricing"],"requirementIds":["req-1","req-3"]},{"heading":"Functional requirements","body":"The product must connect systems, normalize data, detect mismatches, and route approval-ready summaries to finance users.","sourceIds":["src_competitors"],"requirementIds":["req-1","req-2","req-3"]},{"heading":"Non-goals","body":"The first release will not replace accounting systems or support every payment processor.","sourceIds":[],"requirementIds":[]},{"heading":"Risks","body":"Data quality, integration mapping, security, and retention risk must be handled explicitly.","sourceIds":["src_risks"],"requirementIds":["req-1","req-2","req-3"]}],"risks":["Connector scope could expand too quickly.","Financial records require careful access controls."],"openQuestions":["Which mismatch reason codes are required at launch?"]}
{"scores":{"completeness":5,"internalConsistency":5,"grounding":5,"technicalSoundness":4,"actionability":5},"passed":true,"notes":"The PRD is grounded and requirement IDs are stable."}

{"title":"TRD - Finance Reconciliation Copilot","requirements":[],"sections":[{"heading":"Architecture overview","body":"The architecture uses connector workers, a normalized reconciliation store, mismatch services, and approval APIs that trace to the PRD.","sourceIds":[],"requirementIds":["req-1","req-2","req-3"]},{"heading":"Data model","body":"Data entities include accounts, source records, reconciliation runs, mismatch explanations, approval summaries, and audit events.","sourceIds":[],"requirementIds":["req-1","req-2","req-3"]},{"heading":"Integration boundaries","body":"Integration adapters isolate Shopify, Stripe, and QuickBooks data mapping so each connector can fail independently.","sourceIds":["src_risks"],"requirementIds":["req-1"]},{"heading":"Security and privacy","body":"Security requires least privilege, encryption, retention controls, and immutable audit logs for financial records.","sourceIds":["src_risks"],"requirementIds":["req-3"]},{"heading":"Mismatch engine","body":"The mismatch engine compares normalized records and emits explainable reason codes for PRD review workflows.","sourceIds":["src_competitors"],"requirementIds":["req-2"]}],"risks":["Connector API limits may delay reconciliation runs."],"openQuestions":["Which storage engine is preferred for immutable audit events?"]}

{"scores":{"completeness":5,"internalConsistency":5,"grounding":5,"technicalSoundness":5,"actionability":5},"passed":true,"notes":"The TRD covers the PRD requirements and includes architecture, data, security, and integration detail."}

{"title":"Build plan - Finance Reconciliation Copilot","requirements":[],"sections":[{"heading":"Milestone 1 - Connector foundation","body":"Build the connector sequence for Shopify, Stripe, and QuickBooks with acceptance tests against fixture records and PRD traceability.","sourceIds":[],"requirementIds":["req-1"]},{"heading":"Milestone 2 - Reconciliation and mismatch detection","body":"Implement normalized data matching, reason codes, risk flags, and acceptance checks for mismatch accuracy.","sourceIds":["src_competitors"],"requirementIds":["req-2"]},{"heading":"Milestone 3 - Approval workflow","body":"Ship approval summaries, reviewer decisions, exportable evidence, and audit events with PRD coverage.","sourceIds":["src_pricing"],"requirementIds":["req-3"]},{"heading":"Milestone 4 - Security hardening","body":"Verify least privilege, encryption, retention controls, and audit-log review before launch.","sourceIds":["src_risks"],"requirementIds":["req-1","req-2","req-3"]},{"heading":"Release sequence and risks","body":"The sequence reduces integration risk first, then validates workflow acceptance and operational readiness.","sourceIds":[],"requirementIds":["req-1","req-2","req-3"]}],"risks":["Unclear connector permissions could slow release.","Mismatch precision must be tested before customer data is trusted."],"openQuestions":["Who approves launch readiness for financial data handling?"]}
